title: Enhancer remodeling by OTX2 directs specification and patterning of mammalian definitive endoderm.
fluorescent_reporters: SOX17, FOXA2, SOX2, CXCR4, PDGFRA, OTX2-EGFP, HA
key_perturbation: OTX2 degradation via dTAG system during DE specification
key_finding: OTX2 is required for definitive endoderm specification and anterior gut tube patterning through enhancer remodeling and transcriptional regulation of cell adhesion and signaling genes.

title: Detection of developmental toxicity of the anti-COVID-19 drug molnupiravir using gastruloid-based in vitro assays.
journal: Toxicological sciences : an official journal of the Society of Toxicology
key_finding: Gastruloid-based assays reliably detect developmental toxicity of NHC at clinically relevant concentrations comparable to human plasma levels following molnupiravir administration.

title: Stem cell culture conditions affect in vitro differentiation potential and mouse gastruloid formation.
fluorescent_reporters: TBXT-GFP, SOX2-mCherry, TBXT antibody, SOX2 antibody
morphology_metric: aspect ratio, major axis length, minor axis length
key_perturbation: mESC pre-culture conditions (2i pulse timing and duration)
key_finding: Modulating mESC pluripotency state through different pre-culture media combinations affects gastruloid elongation efficiency and reproducibility in a cell line-dependent manner.

title: Gastruloids enable modeling of the earliest stages of human cardiac and hepatic vascularization.
fluorescent_reporters: NKX2-5-eGFP, TNNT2-GFP, BRA, SOX17, SOX2, CDH5-mOrange, TAGLN-CFP
key_perturbation: GSK3β inhibitor (CHIR-99021) dose titration, NOTCH inhibition (DAPT), BMP inhibition (dorsomorphin)
key_finding: Micropatterned hPSCs produce spatially organized cardiac vascularized organoids with branched, lumenized vascular networks integrated with endocardial, myocardial, epicardial, and neuronal cells, resembling a 6.5-postconception week human embryonic heart.

title: Culture of pluripotent stem cells in microscale droplets modulates differentiation and tissue patterning towards organoids on chip.
key_perturbation: droplet volume optimization and confined microenvironment culture
key_finding: Culturing pluripotent stem cells in 7 µl microscale droplets regulates cell fate decisions and promotes tissue patterning in gastruloids and cardioids through altered molecular diffusion dynamics and morphogen signaling.

title: A modular organization of mammalian gastrulation and the Spemann-Mangold organizer.
key_finding: Gastruloids reveal that the vertebrate body plan is organized into three independent developmental modules corresponding to organizer regions in embryos.

title: Lineage recording in monoclonal gastruloids reveals heritable modes of early development.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
key_perturbation: monoclonal vs polyclonal gastruloid generation; lineage tracing with DNA Typewriter
key_finding: Heterogeneity in gastruloids arises from pre-existing, heritable fluctuations in founder mESC states rather than being solely stochastically induced by Wnt agonist exposure.

title: Dual-patterned pluripotent stem cells self-organize into a human embryo model with extended anterior-posterior patterning.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
key_perturbation: dual anterior-posterior patterning through assembly of FGF2-treated anteriorized and CHIR/RA-treated posteriorized hPSC aggregates
key_finding: Human AP gastruloids generated from dual-patterned hPSC populations self-organize into continuous elongated structures with extended anterior-posterior axis patterning including anterior neural and head structures, achieved with over 90% efficiency without requiring specialized equipment.

title: YAP1 and QSER1 are Key Modulators of Embryonic Signaling Pathways in the Mammalian Epiblast.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
key_finding: YAP1 acts as a gatekeeper of epiblast differentiation by suppressing NODAL and WNT signaling to prevent premature primitive streak formation and ectopic mesendoderm induction.

title: Extended culture of 2D gastruloids to model human mesoderm development.
key_finding: Extended culture of 2D gastruloids to 10 days reveals a phase of highly reproducible morphogenesis between 2-4 days during which directed migration from the primitive streak-like region generates multiple types of spatially organized mesoderm.

title: R-loops shape chromatin architecture to promote balanced lineage allocation during differentiation.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
key_perturbation: Rnaseh1 induction via doxycycline for R-loop depletion
key_finding: R-loops are required to maintain H2A.Z deposition at promoters of developmental genes and are essential for balanced lineage allocation during gastruloid differentiation, though dispensable for mESC self-renewal.

title: In preprints: exploring developmental robustness and timing with gastruloids.
key_finding: not reported

title: The people behind the papers - Tina Balayo, André Dias and David Turner.
key_finding: Gastruloids are cultured in N2B27 basal medium which can be either home-made or commercially sourced, and differences between formulations in gastruloid development are being investigated.

title: Dissecting infant leukemia developmental origins with a hemogenic gastruloid model.
fluorescent_reporters: Kdr(Flk1)-GFP, Sox17-GFP, T/Bra::GFP, Rosa26-BFP, CD144, C-Kit, CD41, CD45
key_perturbation: MNX1 oncogene enforced expression to model infant AML with t(7;12) translocation
key_finding: Hemogenic gastruloids recapitulate yolk sac-like and aorta-gonadal-mesonephros-like waves of blood progenitor specification, and identify the endothelial-to-hematopoietic transition as the transformation target of MNX1 in infant acute myeloid leukemia.

title: Derivation of cardiomyocyte-propelled motile aggregates from stem cells.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
cell_line: T-Brachyury eGFP mESC, NKX2-5 emGFP mESC, T-Brachyury_Sox17 Strawberry mESC
fluorescent_reporters: Fluo-4, cTnT, NKX2-5 emGFP, T-Brachyury eGFP, T-Brachyury_Sox17 Strawberry
morphology_metric: track length, contraction area, tissue displacement per contraction
key_perturbation: cardiogenic factor optimization (Activin/BMP4/VEGF for mesoderm induction; FGF2/10/VEGF for cardiogenesis); synthetic organizer localization of contractile areas
key_finding: Mouse embryonic stem cells can be driven via a continuous developmental trajectory to form cardiobots—aggregates with high contractility that support contraction-propelled motility—with optimized protocols achieving contraction area coverage of up to 100% by day 10.

title: Gastrula-Premarked Posterior Enhancer Primes Posterior Tissue Development Through Cross-Talk with TGF-β Signaling Pathway.
key_finding: A gastrula pre-marked enhancer (p-Enh) regulates posterior tissue development through dual mechanisms: in cis regulation of Cdx2 and in trans modulation of TGF-β signaling via enhancer RNA, with p-Enh-KO leading to embryonic lethality and severe posterior tissue growth failure.

title: Development of large-scale gastruloid array to identify aberrant developmental phenotypes.
key_finding: A novel automated microraft array technology was developed for image-based screening and sorting of single gastruloids with heterogeneous patterning behavior, enabling detection of unique phenotypes in euploid and aneuploid gastruloids.

title: The Modulation of Cell Plasticity by Budesonide: Beyond the Metabolic and Anti-Inflammatory Actions of Glucocorticoids.
key_finding: Budesonide stabilizes cell-cell adhesions, preventing embryonic stem cell differentiation and inhibiting the development of 3D gastruloids through epigenetic changes and transcriptome modifications.

title: Endogenous FGFs drive ERK-dependent cell fate patterning in 2D human gastruloids.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
fluorescent_reporters: pERK, TBXT, SOX2, OCT3/4, NANOG, BrdU, cleaved Cas3
key_perturbation: FGF/ERK signaling inhibition and activation; FGF4, FGF8, FGF17 knockdown
key_finding: Endogenous FGFs drive ERK-dependent primitive streak-like cell differentiation in 2D human gastruloids, with FGF4 and FGF17 expressed in the PS-like ring and ERK activity restricted spatially by basolateral FGF receptor localization.

title: Gastruloid patterning reflects division of labor among biased stem cell clones.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
morphology_metric: elongated vs multiaxial vs no axis classification
key_perturbation: single-cell cloning and chimeric aggregate mixing to test effects of ESC population heterogeneity
key_finding: Heterogeneity among mES cell clones actively supports reproducible gastruloid self-organization, with individual clones exhibiting consistent spatial propensities that divide labor for proper axial elongation and tissue organization.

title: Optogenetic Rescue Reveals Spatiotemporal Rules of Germ-Layer Patterning.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
key_perturbation: Optogenetic WNT activation with variable temporal onset and duration; BMP4 and IWP2 co-treatment
key_finding: Temporal dynamics of WNT signaling, not just its presence or concentration, are critical for proper germ layer patterning in human 2D gastruloids, with both onset timing and duration determining mesoderm differentiation and spatial organization.

title: Embryotoxicity analysis of anti-arrhythmia drugs amiodarone, dronedarone, and their metabolites using 3D gastruloid models.
base_media: MEM-α with 10% serum (mouse), 80% MEM-α + 20% 5X mTeSR1 supplement (human)
morphology_metric: elongation distortion index (EDI), aspect ratio, area
key_perturbation: amiodarone, dronedarone, and metabolite exposure dose titration
key_finding: Amiodarone and dronedarone cause dose-dependent reduction in gastruloid growth and elongation, with LOAELs comparable to clinically relevant plasma concentrations, demonstrating gastruloid utility in detecting embryotoxicity and potential species differences in developmental sensitivity.

title: Cooperative short- and long-range interactions enable robust symmetry breaking and axis formation.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
key_finding: Self-organized symmetry breaking robustly emerges in human gastruloids cultured in suspension with CHIR99021 stimulation, showing reproducible elongation and segregation of progenitor populations into distinct domains by 72 hours.

title: ETVs dictate hPSC differentiation by tuning biophysical properties.
fluorescent_reporters: SOX2, BRA, ISL1, OCT3/4, NANOG, KLF4, ETV1, ETV4, ETV5
key_perturbation: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of ETV1, ETV4, and ETV5 genes
key_finding: ETVs dictate hPSC differentiation and germ layer organization in gastruloids by tuning cell adhesion strength through regulation of cell-cell contact genes and the PI3K/AKT signaling pathway.

title: Non-cell-autonomous control of mouse gastruloid development by the ultra-conserved lncRNA T-UCstem1.
fluorescent_reporters: Oct4, Nanog, Sox2, Brachyury, Cdx2, Sox17, E-Cadherin
key_finding: T-UCstem1 is essential for proper gastruloid elongation; its silencing prevents elongation and increases morphological variability while maintaining pluripotency, an effect rescuable by increasing CHIR concentration, suggesting T-UCstem1 controls gastruloid development non-cell-autonomously through modulation of the WNT inhibitor DKK-1.

title: The people behind the papers - Judith Pineau, Jerome Wong-Ng and Thomas Gregor.
key_finding: Mechanical constraint has a strong influence on transcriptional patterning and morphogenesis in gastruloids.

title: Identifying cross-lineage dependencies of cell-type-specific regulators in mouse gastruloids.
key_perturbation: CDX2 knockout and MSGN1 ablation with chimeric analysis
key_finding: CDX2 and MSGN1 are critical regulators of spinal cord and mesoderm development in mouse gastruloids, with inter-tissue communication between MSGN1-expressing and wild-type cells necessary for spinal cord formation.

title: Advances in engineered models of peri-gastrulation.
key_finding: 3D gastruloid models recapitulate spatial organization and developmental processes of early embryos through orchestration of signaling pathways like WNT, BMP, and NODAL, providing insights into symmetry breaking, cell polarization, and germ layer formation in human development.

title: Fine-tuning mechanical constraints reveals uncoupled patterning and gene expression programs in murine gastruloids.
morphology_metric: medial axis length, elongation index, straightness ratio
key_perturbation: hydrogel stiffness titration (0.7-1.5 mM concentrations; 1-300 Pa), embedding timing variation
key_finding: Gastruloids embedded in ultra-soft hydrogels (<1.0 mM) elongate robustly while maintaining AP axis patterning and transcriptional profiles, revealing decoupling of transcriptional programs from mechanical constraints and polarization.

title: N2B27 media formulations influence gastruloid development.
key_perturbation: N2B27 media formulation comparison (HM-N2B27 vs NDiff227)
key_finding: Variations in N2B27 media formulations significantly influence gastruloid morphometrics, developmental timing, gene expression, and cell fate specification.

title: Morphogenetic constraints in the development of gastruloids: Implications for mouse gastrulation.
key_finding: Gastruloid size affects the dynamics and precision of anterior-posterior axial elongation, with axial elongation requiring active cellular contractility, planar cell polarity, and adhesion, while smaller gastruloids initiate elongation earlier correlated with earlier Brachyury polarisation.

title: Optimization of Gastruloid Pre-culture Conditions for Reproducible Germ Layer Composition.
key_finding: A detailed pre-culture protocol optimized for gastruloid generation from mESCs provides a workflow for selecting optimal conditions and evaluating gastruloid formation outcome to facilitate reproducible generation from any cell line.

title: Single-cell spatial mapping reveals reproducible cell type organization and spatially-dependent gene expression in gastruloids.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
key_finding: Single-cell spatial mapping reveals consistent cell type organization and spatial gene expression patterns across gastruloids, with reproducible positioning of neuromesodermal precursors, mesoderm, and neural tissues despite substantial variation in morphological dimensions.

title: In preprints: gastruloids get (a)head.
key_finding: not reported

title: The emergence of human primordial germ cell-like cells in stem cell-derived gastruloids.
key_perturbation: CHIR99021 dose titration (0.00, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0 μM); ISL1 mutation with BMP rescue
key_finding: Human ESCs self-organize into gastruloids that form all three germ layers and contain emerging human primordial germ cell-like cells, with axial mesoderm-derived BMP signaling required for PGCLC specification.

title: Signaling switches: Metabolism regulates gastruloid self-organization.
key_perturbation: metabolic modulation (glycolysis vs oxidative phosphorylation balance)
key_finding: Metabolic activity, specifically the balance between glycolysis and oxidative phosphorylation, regulates cell fate decisions during gastruloid self-organization from mouse embryonic stem cells.

title: Establishment of human gastrulating stem cells with the capacity of stable differentiation into multiple gastrulating cell types.
key_finding: Human gastrulating stem cells self-assemble into gastruloid-like structures modeling Carnegie Stage 7 human embryo gastrulation and can be used to study developmental defects and drug teratogenicity.

title: An Optimized Protocol for the Extended Culture of Gastruloids from Mouse Embryonic Stem Cells.
key_perturbation: Matrigel embedding at 96 h for extended culture
key_finding: An optimized protocol enables reproducible generation of gastruloids with derivatives of all three germ layers and extends culture to 168 h post-aggregation.

title: Exploring and validating the marmoset as a primate model for chromosomal instability in early development.
key_finding: Marmoset pluripotent stem cells exhibit chromosomal instability and recapitulate key phenotypic traits of early embryonic cells, providing a model system for studying aneuploidy during early primate development.

title: Cadherins modulate the self-organizing potential of pseudo-embryos.
key_finding: Cadherin switching, specifically E-cadherin repression by Snai1 and N-cadherin inactivation, is critical for gastruloid self-organization and morphogenetic competence to form embryo-like structures with proper rostro-caudal somite patterning.

title: Towards an integrated view and understanding of embryonic signalling during murine gastrulation.
key_finding: A review discussing similarities and differences between gastruloids and real embryos from a signaling perspective, with focus on gene regulatory networks underlying signaling pathway interactions during gastrulation.

title: Complex aneuploidy triggers autophagy and p53-mediated apoptosis and impairs the second lineage segregation in human preimplantation embryos.
fluorescent_reporters: HSP70, SQSTM1/p62, LC3B, OCT3/4, phosphor-p53 S15, GATA4, DRAM, phospho-histone H2A.X Ser139, NANOG, CaspGLOW Fluorescein Active Caspase-8, CellEvent Caspase-3/7 Green
key_perturbation: Reversine-induced complex aneuploidy; comparison of euploid versus aneuploid human preimplantation embryos
key_finding: Complex aneuploidy in human preimplantation embryos triggers autophagy and p53-mediated apoptosis independent of DNA damage, with effects dependent on the number of imbalanced genes, and impairs the second lineage segregation through reduced cell numbers in the trophectoderm and defective primordial endoderm formation.

title: Embryonic Spinal Cord Innervation in Human Trunk Organogenesis Gastruloids: Cardiac Versus Enteric Customization and Beyond.
key_perturbation: trunk-biased gastruloid protocols with cardiac versus enteric customization
key_finding: Trunk-biased human gastruloids enable self-organized spinal neurogenesis coupled with organogenesis, permitting customizable neural patterning for cardiac or enteric nervous system formation in a multi-lineage, embryo-like context.

title: The proteomic landscape and temporal dynamics of mammalian gastruloid development.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
key_finding: Quantitative proteomics and phosphoproteomics reveal biologically coherent temporal shifts across human and mouse gastruloid development, with conserved shifts in metabolic pathways and developmental markers despite modest late-stage correlation between species.

title: BPA Exposure Affects Mouse Gastruloids Axial Elongation by Perturbing the Wnt/β-Catenin Pathway.
key_perturbation: BPA exposure during Wnt/β-catenin pathway activation
key_finding: BPA exposure arrests gastruloid axial elongation by perturbing the Wnt/β-catenin pathway and reducing β-catenin protein expression.

title: Symbolic recording of signalling and cis-regulatory element activity to DNA.
key_finding: ENGRAM is a multiplex genomic recording strategy that enables biologically conditional recording of signal-specific barcodes to DNA, allowing measurement of past cellular states and integration of signaling activity over time.

title: Coordination between endoderm progression and mouse gastruloid elongation controls endodermal morphotype choice.
key_finding: Coordination between endoderm progression and gastruloid elongation controls endodermal morphotype choice and can reduce morphogenetic variability in vitro.

title: The fusion of physics and biology in early mammalian embryogenesis.
key_finding: Biomechanics intertwining physical forces and biological processes is critical in cell fate decisions and tissue patterning during mammalian embryogenesis, with in vitro models like gastruloids beginning to faithfully recapitulate human development stages.

title: Three-dimensional stem cell models of mammalian gastrulation.
journal: BioEssays : news and reviews in molecular, cellular and developmental biology
key_finding: Pluripotent stem cell models, particularly gastruloids, are emerging as useful tools to understand mammalian gastrulation and address open questions difficult to tackle with embryo studies.

title: CDX2 dose-dependently influences the gene regulatory network underlying human extraembryonic mesoderm development.
key_perturbation: CDX2 dose titration (WT, CDX2-Het, CDX2-KO via CRISPR/Cas9)
key_finding: CDX2 dose-dependently influences gene regulatory networks underlying extraembryonic mesoderm development, with over two-thirds of misregulated genes in CDX2-Het also misregulated in CDX2-KO, particularly affecting WNT and BMP signaling pathways, cell adhesion, and cytoskeletal regulators.

title: Retinoid-enhanced human gastruloids.
key_finding: not reported

title: The shapes of elongating gastruloids are consistent with convergent extension driven by a combination of active cell crawling and differential adhesion.
key_perturbation: ROCK inhibition (Y-27632), computational modeling of differential adhesion and cell crawling mechanisms
key_finding: Gastruloid elongation requires a combination of active cell crawling and differential adhesion, as differential adhesion alone is insufficient to explain the observed complex shape distributions.

title: Validation of a mouse 3D gastruloid-based embryotoxicity assay in reference to the ICH S5(R3) guideline chemical exposure list.
morphology_metric: area, elongation distortion index (EDI), aspect ratio (AR)
key_perturbation: ICH S5(R3) reference chemical exposure titration across 32 pharmaceutical drugs and metabolites
key_finding: The mouse P19C5 gastruloid assay demonstrated sensitivity comparable to in vivo rodent data within an 8-fold concentration margin for 18 of 24 reference drugs (75%), validating it as an effective non-animal method for developmental and reproductive toxicity assessment.

title: Induction and in silico staging of human gastruloids with neural tube, segmented somites & advanced cell types.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
key_finding: A discontinuous regimen of retinoic acid robustly induces human gastruloids with a neural tube and segmented somites, achieving 89% efficiency of embryo-like morphological structures.

title: Genome-Wide CRISPR Screen Identifies an NF2-Adherens Junction Mechanistic Dependency for Cardiac Lineage.
key_finding: NF2 is essential for early cardiomyocyte lineage specification through FERM domain-dependent formation of an AMOT-NF2-YAP scaffold complex at adherens junctions that sequesters YAP and relieves regulatory roadblocks during epithelial-mesenchymal transition.

title: Human peri-gastruloids: a significant advancement in embryology research.
key_finding: Peri-gastruloids comprise both embryonic and extraembryonic tissues, mirroring developmental events from post-implantation through early organogenesis including amniotic and yolk sac cavity emergence and bilaminar to trilaminar embryonic disc progression.

title: Mannose controls mesoderm specification and symmetry breaking in mouse gastruloids.
fluorescent_reporters: T/Bra-HCR, Sox2-HCR, Slc2a1-HCR, Slc2a3-HCR, Wnt3a-HCR, Etv4-HCR, Lef1-HCR, Hoxc6-HCR, Sox1-HCR, Axin2-HCR, Glut1-immunostaining, Glut3-immunostaining, DAPI
morphology_metric: ratio of major to minor axes
key_perturbation: 2-DG glycolysis inhibition; mannose supplementation; glucose concentration titration
key_finding: Mannose, which competes with 2-DG in N-glycosylation, is required for mesoderm specification and gastruloid elongation, acting through the Wnt signaling pathway via proper glycosylation of Frzb.

title: Recording morphogen signals reveals mechanisms underlying gastruloid symmetry breaking.
key_perturbation: CHIR99021 pulse with Nodal/BMP signal recording
key_finding: Gastruloid symmetry breaking occurs through a cell sorting mechanism where spontaneous Nodal and BMP signals create intrinsic heterogeneity that determines anterior-posterior cell fates before morphological polarization, followed by cell migration that sorts distinct populations into a single Wnt-active posterior pole.

title: Polarization of organoids by bioengineered symmetry breaking.
key_finding: Engineering techniques for controlling organoid polarization require improved temporal controllability alongside spatial regulation, with optogenetics, chemigenetics, and context-controllable hydrogels as promising approaches for better mimicking developmental symmetry breaking.

title: An explainable map of human gastruloid morphospace reveals gastrulation failure modes and predicts teratogens.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
morphology_metric: azimuthal signal distribution, spatial distribution of cell fate markers
key_perturbation: drug screen with 210 compounds from Stem Cell Signaling Compound Library
key_finding: High-throughput perturbation mapping identifies 12 distinct morphological phenotypes of human gastruloids and reveals that cell density and SOX2 stability are key parameters determining canonical germ layer patterning outcomes.

title: HBO1 determines SMAD action in pluripotency and mesendoderm specification.
key_perturbation: HBO1 gene knockout and inducible overexpression
key_finding: HBO1, a histone acetyltransferase, determines SMAD action and is required for pluripotency maintenance in human ESCs; HBO1-deficient hESCs spontaneously differentiate towards neuroectoderm fate rather than maintaining pluripotency.

title: Gastruloids are competent to specify both cardiac and skeletal muscle lineages.
fluorescent_reporters: cTnT, VEGFR2, E-cadherin, Hoxc4, Mesp1, Isl1, Tbx1, Tnnt2, Tcf21, Myod, Myf5
key_perturbation: extended culture protocol to day 11 with CHIR99021 pulse and cardiogenic factors (bFGF, VEGF, ascorbic acid) from day 4-7
key_finding: Gastruloids cultured for 11 days robustly express cardiopharyngeal mesoderm markers and differentiate into both cardiomyocyte and skeletal myoblast lineages with spatio-temporal gene expression patterns similar to mouse embryos.

title: TBXT dose sensitivity and the decoupling of nascent mesoderm specification from EMT progression in 2D human gastruloids.
key_perturbation: TBXT dosage reduction (wild-type vs heterozygous vs null)
key_finding: TBXT dosage directly influences the temporal progression of epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition without compromising nascent mesoderm differentiation, demonstrating that EMT progression can be decoupled from mesodermal identity acquisition.

title: Transcriptional remodeling by OTX2 directs specification and patterning of mammalian definitive endoderm.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
fluorescent_reporters: SOX17, FOXA2, SOX2, OCT4, T, EGFP
key_perturbation: OTX2 acute degradation via dTAG system
key_finding: OTX2 is required for definitive endoderm specification and anterior endoderm patterning, functioning as a direct regulator of enhancers controlling signaling, cell adhesion, and gut tube development.

title: Retinoic acid induces human gastruloids with posterior embryo-like structures.
key_finding: Early retinoic acid pulse combined with Matrigel robustly induces human gastruloids with posterior embryo-like structures including neural tube and segmented somites, advancing beyond conventional human gastruloids.

title: Early autonomous patterning of the anteroposterior axis in gastruloids.
fluorescent_reporters: T::GFP, Sox2, Foxa2, Aldh1a2, Eomes, SIR-DNA
key_finding: Autonomously patterning gastruloids establish anteroposterior axis through T polarization by 48 hours post-aggregation, before CHIR99021 stimulation, with concomitant spatial segregation of T+ posterior mesoderm and Sox2+ anterior neuroectoderm domains.

title: Deciphering lineage specification during early embryogenesis in mouse gastruloids using multilayered proteomics.
key_finding: Multilayered proteomics analysis revealed temporal protein expression dynamics during gastruloid differentiation and identified a critical role for ZEB2 in mouse and human somitogenesis.

title: Extended culture of 2D gastruloids to model human mesoderm development.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
fluorescent_reporters: T-neon, SOX2, BRA, ISL1, NANOG, OCT4, EPCAM, ECAD, SNAI1, NCAD, VIM, PODXL, EOMES, MIXL1, TBXT, WNT8A, H2B-RFP, DAPI
morphology_metric: colony thickness, radial position, edge height, colony diameter, radial fluorescence distributions
key_perturbation: BMP4 treatment with signaling manipulation (BMP, Wnt, Nodal balance)
key_finding: Extended 2D micropatterned gastruloids self-organize into a reproducible layered 3D structure over 4 days with inward and outward mesodermal migration from a primitive streak-like ring, recapitulating human gastrulation and mesoderm development.

title: CDX2 dose-dependently influences the gene regulatory network underlying human extraembryonic mesoderm development.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
key_perturbation: CDX2 allelic series (WT, CDX2-Het, CDX2-KO) with BMP4 treatment
key_finding: CDX2 dose-dependently influences gene regulatory networks underlying extraembryonic mesoderm development, with over 2/3 of genes misregulated in CDX2-Het also misregulated in CDX2-KO, particularly affecting WNT5B expression and VEGF signaling pathways critical for vasculogenesis.

title: Modeling post-implantation stages of human development into early organogenesis with stem-cell-derived peri-gastruloids.
key_finding: Human extended pluripotent stem cells self-organize into peri-gastruloids containing both embryonic and extraembryonic tissues that recapitulate critical stages of human peri-gastrulation development including cavity formation, embryonic disc development, and early organogenesis.

title: Recording morphogen signals reveals origins of gastruloid symmetry breaking.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
morphology_metric: center of mass, standard deviation of normalized pixel intensities
key_perturbation: CHIR99021 pulse timing and duration optimization; Wnt signaling pathway monitoring
key_finding: Gastruloid symmetry breaking involves an ordered sequence of signaling events where pre-existing heterogeneity in Nodal/BMP signaling influences the transition of Wnt signaling from uniform to bimodal to polarized states, with early Wnt signaling states predicting future cell fate and position along the anterior-posterior axis.

title: Gastruloid-derived primordial germ cell-like cells develop dynamically within integrated tissues.
cell_line: Blimp1:GFP, Blimp1:mVenus Stella:eCFP (BVSC), Sox17 −/−, Foxa2 −/−, Bmpr1a −/−, Spry4:Venus, Spry4:Venus Fgf4 −/−
fluorescent_reporters: Blimp1:GFP, Blimp1:mVenus, Stella:eCFP, AP2γ, OCT4, NANOG, FOXA2, SOX17, E-cadherin, EpCAM, PECAM1, DAZL, GATA4
key_perturbation: Signalling pathway perturbation (BMP, Wnt, FGF inhibition) to determine role in PGC specification
key_finding: Gastruloids generate a population of primordial germ cell-like cells (Gld-PGCLCs) that mature to E14.5-equivalent stages through coordinated development with somatic tissues, with Wnt and FGF signalling playing greater roles than BMP in determining germline-to-soma balance.

title: TBXT dose sensitivity and the decoupling of nascent mesoderm specification from EMT progression in 2D human gastruloids.
journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
fluorescent_reporters: SOX2, EOMES, SOX17, CDX2, pSMAD1/5, WNT3a, NODAL, LEFTY, LMNB1-GFP
key_perturbation: TBXT allelic series (WT, TBXT-Het, TBXT-KO) with CRISPR/Cas9 genome editing
key_finding: TBXT dose is required for the timing of EMT and acquisition of mesenchymal character in nascent mesoderm, independent of initial mesodermal identity specification.

title: A New Approach to Generate Gastruloids to Develop Anterior Neural Tissues.
key_finding: Inhibition of Wnt signaling in epiblast-like aggregates establishes extended axial patterning and enables co-derivation of anterior neural progenitors and posterior tissues.

title: Pluripotent Stem Cells as a Model for Human Embryogenesis.
key_finding: Pluripotent stem cells can form 3D structures such as blastoids and gastruloids that simulate early embryonic development stages and enable study of human embryogenesis through examination of cellular interactions and spatial organization.

title: Spatial Single Cell Analysis of Proteins in 2D Human Gastruloids Using Iterative Immunofluorescence.
key_finding: An iterative immunofluorescence protocol enables simultaneous measurement of over 27 proteins in 2D human gastruloids to interrogate spatial cell signaling and fate decisions during pattern formation.

title: Haematopoietic development and HSC formation in vitro: promise and limitations of gastruloid models.
key_finding: Gastruloids are pluripotent stem cell-derived 3D aggregates that self-organise multi-tissue structures and hold potential for understanding haematopoietic stem cell specification and enabling HSC production in vitro.

title: Budesonide Analogues Preserve Stem Cell Pluripotency and Delay 3D Gastruloid Development.
key_finding: CHD-030498, a budesonide analogue, prevents stem cell pluripotency exit and delays 3D gastruloid development at lower doses than budesonide.

title: Control of gastruloid patterning and morphogenesis by the Erk and Akt signaling pathways.
key_perturbation: Erk and Akt signaling pathway manipulation
key_finding: Erk and Akt signaling gradients control gastruloid patterning and morphogenesis, with Erk activation sufficient to induce tissue shape changes and convert gastruloids to mesodermal fates depending on anteroposterior position.

title: Stem cell-derived models of spinal neurulation.
key_finding: Recent in vitro models of human and mouse trunk morphogenesis, such as gastruloids, form spinal neural plate-like or neural tube-like structures and offer new avenues to study normal and pathological neurulation.

title: Modifying gastruloids to dissect mechanisms of tissue-specific induction.
key_finding: This is a review article exploring how minimal models including gastruloids can dissect mechanisms of tissue-specific induction and patterning, and whether self-organization can generate appendicular axes or if experimentally induced organizers are required.

title: The people behind the papers - Christopher Cooke and Naomi Moris.
key_finding: A PGC-like cell population was identified in mouse gastruloids, providing new insight into signals contributing to PGC specification and maturation.

title: Learning the mechanobiology of development from gastruloids.
key_finding: Gastruloids acquire their organization and shape through cell biochemical and mechanical activities, and mechanobiology concepts are essential to understanding gastruloid development.

title: Multimodal characterization of murine gastruloid development.
fluorescent_reporters: DAPI, pAKT2, ALK4, β-catenin, Brachyury, Cdx2, Dppa4, E-cadherin, EGFR, Fibronection, FoxA2, Gata6, Hes1, Ki67, N-cadherin, Nanog, Nestin, Oct3/4, Otx1/2, Otx2, p44/42 MAPK, Pax6, Phospho-p44/42 MAPK, Phospho Smad2, Sox1, Sox2, Tbr2/Eomes, Tuj1
key_perturbation: Wnt activation via CHIR99021 pulse; high-content screen targeting symmetry breaking and patterning events; dual Wnt modulation
key_finding: Gastruloids exhibit spatial heterogeneity of pluripotency exit that causes binary response to Wnt activation, driving radial symmetry breaking, morphological changes, and axial elongation, with dual Wnt modulation enabling generation of gastruloids with anterior neural and endodermal structures.

title: Generation of embryo-like structures from mouse embryonic stem cells treated with a chemical inhibitor of SUMOylation and cultured in microdroplets.
key_finding: Chemical inhibition of SUMOylation in mouse embryonic stem cells cultured in microdroplets generates highly reproducible gastruloids and embryo-like structures with anterior neural and trunk somite-like regions.

title: Gastruloids - a minimalistic model to study complex developmental metabolism.
key_finding: Gastruloids are an ideal reductionist system to uncover mechanistic links between developmental signalling pathways and metabolic networks.

title: The ever-growing world of gastruloids: autogenous models of mammalian embryogenesis.
key_finding: Gastruloids are tractable, scalable in vitro models of mammalian gastrulation that enable study of self-organisation, patterning and fate specification.

title: In Vitro Embryogenesis and Gastrulation Using Stem Cells in Mice and Humans.
key_finding: not reported

title: Gastruloid optimization.
key_finding: Robustness and reproducibility of gastruloids both within and between experiments are key to wide adoption for developmental modeling and drug screening applications.

title: Gastruloids: A Novel System for Disease Modelling and Drug Testing.
key_finding: Gastruloids derived from pluripotent stem cells recapitulate key aspects of gastrula-stage embryos and provide a tractable in vitro tool suitable for high-throughput investigation of developmental and environmental aberrations.

title: A combined human gastruloid model of cardiogenesis and neurogenesis.
key_perturbation: growth factor supplementation for cardiogenesis (VEGF and ascorbic acid addition at 48h post-aggregation)
key_finding: Modified growth factor supplementation at 48 hours post-aggregation directs EMLO gastruloids toward cardiogenesis, generating elongated structures with chamber-like cavities, spontaneous contractility, and integrated neurogenesis by day 7.

title: Circadian key component CLOCK/BMAL1 interferes with segmentation clock in mouse embryonic organoids.
journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
fluorescent_reporters: Hes7-promoter-driven luciferase, Per2-luciferase, Uncx4.1 HCR probe
key_finding: Premature expression of circadian key components CLOCK/BMAL1 severely interferes with the ultradian rhythm of the segmentation clock in embryonic organoids and disrupts the striped structure of somite markers.

title: Hypoxia induces an early primitive streak signature, enhancing spontaneous elongation and lineage representation in gastruloids.
key_finding: Hypoxia induces an early primitive streak signature, enhancing spontaneous elongation and improving lineage representation in gastruloids, particularly through enhanced endoderm formation and mesodermal derivatives.

title: Nodal is a short-range morphogen with activity that spreads through a relay mechanism in human gastruloids.
fluorescent_reporters: cNodal-mCitrine, Lefty, BRA, SOX2, pSmad2/3, mCherry-CAAX, CFP-H2B
key_perturbation: Nodal/Lefty knockout and juxtaposition experiments to determine Nodal signaling range
key_finding: Nodal is a short-range morphogen that does not spread more than one cell diameter and propagates through a transcriptional relay mechanism, while Lefty protein spreads over 6-8 cell tiers but functions at short range during gastruloid patterning.

title: A gastruloid model of the interaction between embryonic and extra-embryonic cell types.
key_perturbation: XEN cell co-aggregation with mESCs at varying ratios (1:1, 1:2, 1:3, 1:4, 1:5, 0:1); CHIR99021 GSK3 inhibition
key_finding: Co-aggregation of mESCs with XEN cells (extraembryonic) cells at a 1:3 ratio induces the formation of organized neuroepithelial structures with columnar epithelia and lumina, whereas mESCs alone form disorganized SOX2-positive cells.

title: Stabilization of cell-cell adhesions prevents symmetry breaking and locks in pluripotency in 3D gastruloids.
key_perturbation: budesonide treatment to inhibit symmetry breaking
key_finding: Budesonide, a glucocorticoid, prevents gastruloid elongation and locks cells in pluripotency by stabilizing E-CADHERIN-mediated cell-cell adhesions and blocking differentiation independently of glucocorticoid receptor signaling.

title: Gastruloids: Pluripotent stem cell models of mammalian gastrulation and embryo engineering.
key_finding: Gastruloids are ES cell aggregates that undergo controlled proliferation, symmetry breaking, and specification of all three germ layers, but reveal a disconnect between cell type specific gene expression and tissue morphogenesis.

title: Approaches to benchmark and characterize in vitro human model systems.
key_finding: This Spotlight discusses the state of current in vitro model systems including gastruloids and organoids, efforts to create large publicly available atlases of developing human tissues, and how these data are being used to improve in vitro models.

title: Generation of Gastruloids from Epiblast-Like Cells.
key_finding: Gastruloids can be generated from epiblast-like cells induced from ESCs, and gastruloid formation efficiency discriminates different states of mouse pluripotency.

title: Gastruloids as in vitro models of embryonic blood development with spatial and temporal resolution.
key_perturbation: VEGF, bFGF, and ascorbic acid supplementation to support hematopoietic development
key_finding: Gastruloids express key hematopoiesis-related genes in a temporally relevant fashion that recapitulates embryonic blood development, with CD41+ blood progenitor cells emerging in close proximity to the vascular network and endodermal compartment, and showing multipotent clonogenic potential and embryonic hemoglobin expression.

title: Capturing Transitional Pluripotency through Proline Metabolism.
key_finding: Proline-induced cells represent a metastable naïve-to-primed intermediate pluripotency state with reversible E-cadherin relocalization and capacity for 3D gastruloid development.

title: Chimeric 3D gastruloids - a versatile tool for studies of mammalian peri-gastrulation development.
key_perturbation: chimeric gastruloids with Tbx brachyury deletion and inducible Eomes expression
key_finding: Chimeric gastruloids combining fluorescently labelled cells of different genotypes reveal brachyury and Eomes functions including cardiac fate instruction, posterior axial extension, tissue sorting preferences of mutant cells, and cell non-autonomous effects on Wnt3a patterning.

title: Human stem cell-derived neurons and neural circuitry therapeutics: Next frontier in spinal cord injury repair.
key_finding: NMP-derived spinal motor neurons generated from hiPSCs demonstrate robust survival when transplanted into spinal cord injury sites, and in vitro gastruloid models are advancing as tools for studying trunk nervous system integration.

title: Cell-state transitions and collective cell movement generate an endoderm-like region in gastruloids.
key_perturbation: CHIR99021 (Wnt agonist) pulse for 24 hours starting at 48 hours post-plating
key_finding: Chiron-exposed gastruloids develop polarized E-cadherin and T-Brachyury expression at one pole, with E-cadherin-positive cells segregating to form an endoderm-like region through coordinated cell movements and state transitions, distinct from mesoderm-like T-Bra-expressing cells.

title: YAP1 regulates the self-organized fate patterning of hESC-derived gastruloids.
fluorescent_reporters: SOX2, OTX2, BRACHYURY, SOX17, OCT4, CDX2, YAP1
key_finding: YAP1 regulates the correct specification and allocation of the three germinal layers in hESC-derived 2D gastruloids by restricting Nodal signaling.

title: SEPHS1 is dispensable for pluripotency maintenance but indispensable for cardiac differentiation in mouse embryonic stem cells.
key_finding: SEPHS1 deficiency impaired gastruloid aggregation and differentiation into germ layers, particularly affecting cardiac lineage development, while having little effect on pluripotency maintenance.

title: An automated do-it-yourself system for dynamic stem cell and organoid culture in standard multi-well plates.
key_perturbation: CHIR99021 pulse duration titration (varying Wnt pathway activation timing)
key_finding: Minimum 24-hour CHIR pulse is required and sufficient to induce robust symmetry breaking and gastruloid elongation, with extended Wnt pathway activation correlating with enhanced mesodermal differentiation including cardiac tissue formation.

title: Generation of human elongating multi-lineage organized cardiac gastruloids.
key_perturbation: FGF2, HGF, IGF1, Y-27632 signaling with cardiogenic factors (FGF2, VEGF, ascorbic acid)
key_finding: Human elongating multi-lineage organized (EMLOC) gastruloids with integrated neurons and multi-chambered contractile cardiac tissue can be generated using timed exposure to defined signaling factors.

title: Autonomy in the Development of Stem Cell-Derived Embryoids: Sprouting Blastocyst-Like Cysts, and Ethical Implications.
key_finding: Ethical aspects of organismic wholeness and autonomy in stem cell-derived embryoids warrant greater attention in regulatory discussions regarding this technology.

title: Gastruloids generated without exogenous Wnt activation develop anterior neural tissues.
fluorescent_reporters: SOX1-GFP, T/BRA-mCherry, TLC-mCherry, AR8-mCherry, SOX2, NANOG, OCT4, SOX17
key_perturbation: WNT inhibition with XAV939 vs. no inhibition; cell number per aggregate (25 vs. 100 cells/well)
key_finding: Gastruloids generated without exogenous Wnt activation in EPI medium develop anterior neural tissues (forebrain, midbrain, hindbrain) when WNT signaling is inhibited early with XAV939, contrasting with conventional gastruloids that primarily form mesendodermal tissues.

title: Author Correction: Depletion of aneuploid cells in human embryos and gastruloids.
key_finding: not reported

title: Modeling mammalian trunk development in a dish.
key_finding: Embedding of gastruloids in Matrigel enables self-organization into trunk-like structures with bilateral somites, neural tube-like structure, gut tissue, and primordial germ cell-like cells.

title: Cardiac Organoids and Gastruloids to Study Physio-Pathological Heart Development.
journal: Journal of cardiovascular development and disease
key_finding: not reported

title: Mouse gastruloids take heart.
key_finding: not reported

title: Gastruloids: Embryonic Organoids from Mouse Embryonic Stem Cells to Study Patterning and Development in Early Mammalian Embryos.
key_finding: Gastruloids are embryonic organoids from mESCs that display distinct, well-organized gene expression domains and axial elongation over 5-7 days, representing a useful system for studying early developmental patterning mechanisms.

title: Bioengineering Self-Organizing Signaling Centers to Control Embryoid Body Pattern Elaboration.
key_perturbation: WNT3A provision via engineered HEK-293 cells
key_finding: Synthetic biology approach using Cdh3-expressing HEK cells producing WNT3A induces localized mesoderm specification and pattern elaboration in embryoid bodies through polarized Wnt/β-catenin pathway activation.

title: Huntingtin CAG expansion impairs germ layer patterning in synthetic human 2D gastruloids through polarity defects.
key_perturbation: HTT CAG expansion length variation (20CAG, 56CAG, 72CAG controls vs. HTT−/− deletion)
key_finding: HTT-CAG expansion impairs germ layer patterning in 2D gastruloids through polarity-dependent defects in TGFβ signaling, causing reduced ectodermal SOX2+ domain size that correlates with CAG length.

title: A Microfluidics Approach for the Functional Investigation of Signaling Oscillations Governing Somitogenesis.
key_perturbation: microfluidic entrainment of signaling oscillations with pathway modulator pulses
key_finding: Microfluidics enables functional investigation of signaling oscillations governing somitogenesis through modulation of oscillation period, phase-relationships, and spatial gradients of pathway modulators, and can be adapted for gastruloids and organoids.

title: In vitro teratogenicity testing using a 3D, embryo-like gastruloid system.
chir_onset_h: 0 (human pre-treatment on day 4 of adherent culture), 48 (mouse between 48-72h)
key_perturbation: ATRA, valproic acid, bosentan, thalidomide, phenytoin, ibuprofen, penicillin G exposure at multiple concentrations
key_finding: Gastruloids can distinguish known teratogens from non-teratogens through morphological and gene expression changes, with human gastruloids showing greater sensitivity to compound exposure than mouse gastruloids.

title: Fate-Patterning of 2D Gastruloids and Ectodermal Colonies Using Micropatterned Human Pluripotent Stem Cells.
key_perturbation: micropatterned human pluripotent stem cell colonies of defined size and shape
key_finding: Micropatterned 2D systems allow study of self-organized patterning of germ layers at gastrulation and medial-lateral ectoderm patterning through intercellular signaling.

title: Co-development of central and peripheral neurons with trunk mesendoderm in human elongating multi-lineage organized gastruloids.
key_perturbation: CHIR and FGF2 pretreatment, SMAD inhibition (LDN 193189 and SB 431542), Hh-Ag1.5 supplementation
key_finding: EMLOs demonstrate reproducible polarized development of interconnected CNS and PNS neurons with trunk mesendoderm and a primitive gut tube that acts as a morphological organizer of neuronal fibers.

title: Gene expression dynamics underlying cell fate emergence in 2D micropatterned human embryonic stem cell gastruloids.
fluorescent_reporters: POU5F1, SOX2, NANOG, GATA3, TFAP2A, T, SOX17, CDH1, CDH2, MESP1, FOXA2, NANOS3, TFAP2C
key_finding: Sequential emergence of seven cell types (EPI-like, ectoderm, mesoderm, endoderm, hPGCLCs, and ExE-like cells) occurs over 44 hours of BMP4 treatment, with prospective ectoderm and GATA3+ cells forming by 12h, mesendoderm precursors by 24h, and mature germ layers by 44h.

title: Membrane Tension Gates ERK-Mediated Regulation of Pluripotent Cell Fate.
key_perturbation: exit from naive pluripotency via withdrawal of 2i+L inhibitors
key_finding: Membrane tension decreases as ESCs exit naive pluripotency, and this decrease in membrane tension gates ERK-mediated differentiation by regulating endocytosis rates independent of cell shape changes.

title: Reaction-diffusion models for morphological patterning of hESCs.
key_finding: Reaction-diffusion models with Gierer-Meinhardt equations identify three distinct regimes for BMP4, Wnt, and Nodal that produce realistic protein concentration dynamics consistent with experimental hESC patterning.

title: Gastruloid Development Competence Discriminates Different States of Pluripotency.
fluorescent_reporters: CDX2, NESTIN, T/BRA, SOX2, SOX17, Ki67
key_perturbation: Cripto genetic ablation and rescue with recombinant CRIPTO or ACTIVIN A protein
key_finding: Gastruloid formation efficiency discriminates between naive and primed pluripotency states, with naive ESCs generating elongated gastruloids while primed EpiSCs fail to do so, and Cripto is essential for symmetry breaking and A-P axis formation in gastruloids.

title: Depletion of aneuploid cells in human embryos and gastruloids.
key_perturbation: aneuploid cell depletion in micropatterned human gastruloids
key_finding: Aneuploid cells are depleted from embryonic germ layers but not extraembryonic tissue in human gastruloids through BMP4-dependent apoptosis, demonstrating an in vitro mechanism for aneuploidy elimination.

title: Human Embryos, Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells, and Organoids: Models to Assess the Effects of Environmental Plastic Pollution.
journal: Frontiers in cell and developmental biology
key_finding: Organoids show potential for assessing effects of environmental plastic pollution on human cells by examining cytotoxic effects, gene expression changes, and ROS production, though limitations including lack of vasculature and immune cells must be addressed.

title: 3D gastruloids: a novel frontier in stem cell-based in vitro modeling of mammalian gastrulation.
key_finding: 3D gastruloids, aggregates of embryonic stem cells, recapitulate key aspects of gastrula-stage embryos and provide a tractable tool to study early mammalian post-implantation development in vitro with potential for high-throughput analysis of normal and pathological embryogenesis.

title: Capturing Cardiogenesis in Gastruloids.
fluorescent_reporters: Hcn4-GFP, Tbx1Cre-RFP, Flk1-GFP, Mesp1-GFP, Gata6-Venus, Gata4, GFP, Brachyury, CD31, cardiac troponin T, E-Cadherin
key_perturbation: N2B27+++ medium optimization with bFGF, VEGF165, and L-ascorbic acid phosphate from 96-144 h
key_finding: Gastruloids can be stimulated to form a cardiac primordium with organized spatial arrangement of early cardiac progenitors and first and second heart field compartmentalization through optimized culture conditions combining cardiogenic growth factors.

title: Generation of Mouse Pluripotent Stem Cell-derived Trunk-like Structures: An in vitro Model of Post-implantation Embryogenesis.
key_finding: Addition of low percentage Matrigel to gastruloids results in formation of trunk-like structures with neural tube flanked by bilateral somites, recapitulating mid-gestational mouse embryogenesis.

title: Stem Cell Neurodevelopmental Solutions for Restorative Treatments of the Human Trunk and Spine.
key_finding: Human stem cell developmental neurotechnologies offer promising approaches for understanding and treating trunk and spine disorders including neural tube defects, neurocristopathies, neuromuscular disorders, and neurodegeneration through disease modeling and cell replacement therapies.

title: High-resolution transcriptional and morphogenetic profiling of cells from micropatterned human ESC gastruloid cultures.
fluorescent_reporters: SOX2, OCT3/4, T, SOX17, CDX2, pSMAD1, SOX1, GATA3, GATA6, FOXA2, ZO-1, KRT7, AP-2α, AP-2γ, CDH1, CDH2
key_finding: BMP4 treatment of hESCs cultured on ECM micro-discs for 44 hours reproducibly generates radially-arranged gastruloids containing seven distinct cell types representing three germ layers and extragonadal-like cells with primate-specific characteristics including hPGCLC and cells with amnion signatures.

title: Measuring and Modeling Single-Cell Heterogeneity and Fate Decision in Mouse Embryos.
key_finding: Disentangling the role of heterogeneity in cell fate decision will likely rely on the refined integration of experiments, large-scale omics data, and mathematical modeling, complemented by the use of synthetic embryos and gastruloids as promising in vitro models.

title: Translating Embryogenesis to Generate Organoids: Novel Approaches to Personalized Medicine.
key_finding: Stem cell-based embryo models including gastruloids will be increasingly important for personalized medicine, but efforts are needed to increase yield and reproducibility.

title: Single-cell and spatial transcriptomics reveal somitogenesis in gastruloids.
key_finding: Gastruloids embedded in Matrigel generate somites with correct rostral-caudal patterning that appear sequentially in an anterior-to-posterior direction, demonstrating somitogenesis in vitro with dynamics resembling those in vivo.

title: Gastruloids Gain Muscle: Somite Formation in Embryo-Like Structures.
key_finding: Mouse gastruloids display significant similarities to embryos in positional lineage segregation and somite formation.

title: Methoxyacetic acid inhibits histone deacetylase and impairs axial elongation morphogenesis of mouse gastruloids in a retinoic acid signaling-dependent manner.
base_media: 90% minimum essential medium alpha with nucleosides and GlutaMAX Supplement, 2.5% fetal bovine serum, 7.5% newborn calf serum
key_perturbation: methoxyacetic acid (MAA) dose titration; retinoic acid signaling inhibition with BMS493
key_finding: Methoxyacetic acid impairs axial elongation morphogenesis of mouse gastruloids at teratogenic concentrations in a retinoic acid signaling-dependent manner, possibly through histone deacetylase inhibition.

title: Publisher Correction: Single-cell and spatial transcriptomics reveal somitogenesis in gastruloids.
key_finding: Publisher correction to a prior paper on somitogenesis in gastruloids.

title: An in vitro model of early anteroposterior organization during human development.
key_finding: Human embryonic stem cells generate gastruloids that undergo elongation along an anteroposterior axis with patterned gene expression and somitogenesis features resembling Carnegie-stage-9 embryos.

title: Dissecting the dynamics of signaling events in the BMP, WNT, and NODAL cascade during self-organized fate patterning in human gastruloids.
key_perturbation: NODAL knockout via CRISPR-Cas9, WNT inhibition via IWP2, NODAL pathway inhibition via SB431542
key_finding: BMP signaling initiates dynamic waves of WNT and NODAL signaling that move toward the colony center, with WNT signaling initiating mesodermal differentiation and NODAL signaling up-regulating it, rather than stable signaling gradients underlying primitive streak formation.

title: Chick Models and Human-Chick Organizer Grafts.
key_perturbation: interspecies chimera transplantation of hESC-derived gastruloids into chick embryo
key_finding: Human organizer cells derived from hESC gastruloids self-organize to contribute to notochord-like tissue and indirectly induce host chick cells to generate neural tissue in human-chick chimeras.

title: Mapping cell migrations and fates in a gastruloid model to the human primitive streak.
key_finding: Differing levels of BMP, WNT and NODAL lead to self-organization of human gastruloids into distinct endoderm and mesoderm subpopulations that can be mapped along an anterior-posterior axis, and fate-dependent cell migrations comparable to the mouse embryo were detected using novel cell tracking.

title: WNT signaling memory is required for ACTIVIN to function as a morphogen in human gastruloids.
key_perturbation: WNT signaling pre-treatment followed by ACTIVIN stimulation; SMAD pathway inhibition with SB431542
key_finding: WNT signaling creates a cellular memory that enables human gastruloid cells to respond to ACTIVIN as a morphogen, but ACTIVIN alone is insufficient to induce mesendodermal differentiation without prior WNT exposure.

title: Multi-axial self-organization properties of mouse embryonic stem cells into gastruloids.
key_perturbation: gastrulation-like stimulation and elongation in vitro
key_finding: Small aggregates of mouse ESCs self-organize to generate post-occipital neural, mesodermal and endodermal derivatives with collinear Hox patterns along an antero-posterior axis, revealing an unanticipated self-organizing capacity.

title: The Chick Caudolateral Epiblast Acts as a Permissive Niche for Generating Neuromesodermal Progenitor Behaviours.
key_finding: ES cell-derived NMp-like cells and undifferentiated ES cells display similar potentials to generate neuromesodermal progenitor behaviour in vivo, while this competence is lost in the anterior portion of gastruloids.

title: Anteroposterior polarity and elongation in the absence of extra-embryonic tissues and of spatially localised signalling in gastruloids: mammalian embryonic organoids.
fluorescent_reporters: AR8::mCherry, T/Bra::GFP, GATA6::H2B-Venus, IBRE4::Cerulean, miR-290-mCherry/mir-302-eGFP, Nodal::YFP, Sox17::GFP, TCF/LEF::mCherry, Sox1::GFP
key_finding: Gastruloids develop anteroposterior polarity and elongation characteristic of the embryonic tail domain in the absence of extra-embryonic tissues and spatially localized signaling, with Wnt/β-catenin signaling providing robustness to T/Bra expression polarization.

title: Ethical issues in human organoid and gastruloid research.
key_finding: Research involving human organoids and gastruloids involves ethical issues related to their derivation, maturation, and use, which are not specifically addressed by existing ethics oversight mechanisms.

title: Engineering Ethics and Self-Organizing Models of Human Development: Opportunities and Challenges.
key_finding: Incorporating engineering ethics early in organoid and gastruloid research planning may help clarify the moral status of complex developmental models, though the self-organizing nature of these systems poses challenges.

title: Embryoids, organoids and gastruloids: new approaches to understanding embryogenesis.
key_finding: Embryoids, organoids and gastruloids generated in vitro provide opportunities to explore complex embryological events in a detailed and quantitative manner.

title: A Balance between Secreted Inhibitors and Edge Sensing Controls Gastruloid Self-Organization.
fluorescent_reporters: pSMAD1, YAP/TAZ, ZO-1, WGA, BMPRIA, BMPRII, ActRIB, ActRIIB
key_perturbation: BMP4 dose titration and cell density variation on micropatterned colonies
key_finding: Gastruloid self-organization is controlled by a combination of density-dependent receptor relocalization to the basolateral surface and BMP4-induced secretion of the Noggin inhibitor, which together create spatial restrictions in TGF-β signaling response.

title: Self-Organization of Stem Cell Colonies and of Early Mammalian Embryos: Recent Experiments Shed New Light on the Role of Autonomy vs. External Instructions in Basic Body Plan Development.
key_finding: Self-organization of gastruloids and early mammalian embryos in vitro demonstrates pattern formation and basic body plan development through mechanisms that involve autonomy versus external instructive signals.

title: Signaling Boundary Conditions Drive Self-Organization of Human "Gastruloids".
key_finding: A confined colony of human embryonic stem cells can spontaneously sense its boundary, generating a self-organized TGF-β signaling gradient that patterns it into a tissue resembling a gastrulating embryo.

title: Symmetry breaking, germ layer specification and axial organisation in aggregates of mouse embryonic stem cells.
fluorescent_reporters: Sox1-GFP, Sox17-GFP, Bra-GFP, TCF/LEF-mCherry, TBX6-EYFP, GPI-GFP, Bra, E-Cadherin, Sox17, FoxA2
key_perturbation: N2B27 pre-treatment followed by Act/Chi signalling pulse timing and aggregate size optimization
key_finding: Aggregates of mESCs undergo symmetry breaking and polarization under defined culture conditions with optimal cell number (300±100 cells) and sequential exposure to N2B27 followed by activin A and CHIR99021, resulting in median 30% elongated gastruloids by day 4.

title: Mouse embryonic stem cells self-organize into trunk-like structures with neural tube and somites.
key_perturbation: Tbx6 knockout, chemical modulation of somite formation
key_finding: Mouse ESC aggregates self-organize into trunk-like structures with neural tube and somites that recapitulate post-implantation embryogenesis through similar gene-regulatory programs.

title: Synergy with TGFβ ligands switches WNT pathway dynamics from transient to sustained during human pluripotent cell differentiation.
journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
key_perturbation: WNT signaling with TGFβ ligands (Activin and BMP) co-stimulation
key_finding: β-catenin signaling dynamics switch from transient to sustained upon differentiation of pluripotent stem cells, with TGFβ ligands enhancing the β-catenin response independent of WNT ligand production.

title: Opposing Nodal and Wnt signalling activities govern the emergence of the mammalian body plan
key_finding: Opposing Wnt and Nodal signalling activities govern AP identity. Nodal signalling inversely affects Wnt activity (and vice versa). Wnt drives posterior PS fate (TBXT, CDX2); Nodal drives anterior PS fate (EOMES, FOXA2, LHX1).
